title: BMI Calculator — Body Mass Index with Interpretation
description: Calculate your BMI instantly. Get WHO category, healthy weight range, BMI Prime, and AI-powered health interpretation — free, fast, and accurate.

acceptedAnswer.text: BMI (Body Mass Index) is calculated from your height and weight. Formula: BMI = weight (kg) ÷ height² (m²). WHO defines four categories: Underweight (<18.5), Normal (18.5–24.9), Overweight (25–29.9), and Obese (≥30).
According to the WHO, a healthy BMI for adults is between 18.5 and 24.9.
BMI is a useful screening tool but has limitations. It does not directly measure body fat and does not account for muscle mass, bone density, or fat distribution.
BMI Prime is your BMI divided by 25. A value of 1.0 means you are at the upper limit of normal weight. Below 1.0 is normal or underweight; above 1.0 is overweight or obese.
The Ponderal Index is weight (kg) ÷ height³ (m³). It scales better than BMI for very tall or short individuals. Normal range is approximately 11–14 kg/m³.
